system.description: Sherazade's Gift 'Words like Honey' makes her incredibly persuasive and believable—people trust her instinctively, follow her suggestions, believe her lies. You've learned to see through her narrative manipulation by focusing on actions rather than words. When Sherazade or her Shadows attempt social manipulation, persuasion, or deception against you, you gain +2 to resist (effectively reducing her OL from 7 to 5 for Social Checks targeting you). Additionally, once per Scene, you can point out a contradiction in her story to one NPC: make a Social Check vs her OL 7—on success, that NPC becomes immune to her Words like Honey for this Scene and may turn against her. This represents your ability to find truth buried beneath beautiful lies.
system.requirements: Recognize Sherazade's Words like Honey Gift in action and study how she manipulates others, OR resist her manipulation successfully at least once
system.limitations: Only protects YOU from social manipulation—allies remain vulnerable unless you spend action to free them. Pointing out contradictions may anger Sherazade, causing her to abandon subtlety and attack directly. Some NPCs are so deeply manipulated they refuse to believe evidence even when presented clearly. This Gift doesn't work against her other powers (story imprisonment, Shadow summoning)—only social manipulation.
